description: OpenHands agent execution is represented through its containerized runtime and workspace isolation layer.

description: The OpenHands runtime is represented as using Docker/Moby-style containers as a baseline execution sandbox.

sources.title: OpenHands GitHub repository
sources.quote: The core `openhands` and `agent-server` Docker images are fully MIT-licensed as well.

sources.title: Moby GitHub repository
sources.quote: The Moby project is not intended as a location for support or feature requests for Docker products, but as a place for contributors to work on open source code, fix bugs, and make the code more useful. The releases are supported by the maintainers, community and users, on a best efforts basis only. For customers who want enterprise or commercial support, Docker Desktop and Mirantis Container Runtime are the appropriate products for these use cases.

description: Continue is represented as able to operate inside reproducible development-container workspaces.

sources.title: Dev Containers specification
sources.quote: Beyond repeatable setup, these same development containers provide consistency to avoid environment specific problems across developers and centralized build and test automation services. You can use the open-source CLI reference implementation either directly or integrated into product experiences to use the structured metadata to deliver these benefits. It currently supports integrating with Docker Compose and a simplified, un-orchestrated single container option – so that they can be used as coding environments or for continuous integration and testing.

description: LibreChat self-hosting is represented as commonly deployed through containerized infrastructure.

sources.title: LibreChat GitHub repository
sources.quote: - 🔧 **Code Interpreter API**: - Secure, Sandboxed Execution in Python, Node.js (JS/TS), Go, C/C++, Java, PHP, Rust, and Fortran - Seamless File Handling: Upload, process, and download files directly - No Privacy Concerns: Fully isolated and secure execution

sources.title: Moby GitHub repository
sources.quote: Moby is an open-source project created by Docker to enable and accelerate software containerization.

description: Open WebUI self-hosting is represented as commonly deployed through containerized infrastructure.

sources.title: Open WebUI GitHub repository
sources.quote: If you're experiencing connection issues, it’s often due to the WebUI docker container not being able to reach the Ollama server at 127.0.0.1:11434 (host.docker.internal:11434) inside the container . Use the `--network=host` flag in your docker command to resolve this. Note that the port changes from 3000 to 8080, resulting in the link: `http://localhost:8080`.

description: E2B cloud sandbox infrastructure is represented as a microVM-backed isolation path.

sources.title: E2B infra GitHub repository
sources.note: The linked source does not provide edge-specific evidence for software:e2b-infra sandboxed_by software:firecracker; this relationship is source-needed until a direct quote is collected.

sources.title: Firecracker GitHub repository
sources.quote: Firecracker is an open source virtualization technology that is purpose-built for creating and managing secure, multi-tenant container and function-based services that provide serverless operational models. Firecracker runs workloads in lightweight virtual machines, called microVMs, which combine the security and isolation properties provided by hardware virtualization technology with the speed and flexibility of containers.

description: Daytona is represented as supporting orchestrated workspace deployments on Kubernetes-class infrastructure.
Daytona workspaces are represented as relying on container-style workspace isolation.

sources.title: Daytona GitHub repository
sources.quote: Daytona is a secure and elastic infrastructure runtime for AI-generated code execution and agent workflows. Our open-source platform provides sandboxes, full composable computers with complete isolation, a dedicated kernel, filesystem, network stack, and allocated vCPU, RAM, and disk.

description: Kata Containers is represented as a Kubernetes-compatible VM-backed container runtime option.

sources.title: Kata Containers GitHub repository
sources.quote: Kata Containers uses a single configuration file which contains a number of sections for various parts of the Kata Containers system including the runtime, the agent and the hypervisor.

description: Kubernetes is represented as supporting gVisor-style container sandbox runtimes through runtime integration.

sources.title: gVisor GitHub repository
sources.quote: gVisor includes an [Open Container Initiative (OCI)][oci] runtime called `runsc` that makes it easy to work with existing container tooling. The `runsc` runtime integrates with Docker and Kubernetes, making it simple to run sandboxed containers.

description: Dagger is represented as using container runtime infrastructure for reproducible workflow execution.

sources.title: Dagger GitHub repository
sources.quote: **Local-first**. Once you automate a task with Dagger, it will reliably run on any supported system: your laptop, AI sandbox, CI server, or dedicated cloud infrastructure. The only dependency is a container runtime like Docker.

description: KServe is represented as relying on Kubernetes for model-serving orchestration.

sources.title: KServe GitHub repository
sources.quote: - **Standard Kubernetes Installation**: Compared to Serverless Installation, this is a more **lightweight** installation. However, this option does not support canary deployment and request based autoscaling with scale-to-zero.
